channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Malema sentenced to five years for firearm offences, vows to appeal and lodge complaints against court officials
item.description: EFF leader Julius Malema has been sentenced to five years' direct imprisonment by the KuGompo City magistrate after being found guilty of unlawfully discharging a firearm in public, unlawful possessio...

item.title: Baby names prohibited in different countries due to legal and cultural restrictions
item.description: While the United Kingdom does not impose strict bans on specific baby names, many countries around the world have introduced regulations to protect children from potentially harmful, offensive or inap...

item.title: Raiders defeat Melbourne Storm 26-22 as Simi Sasagi stars in dominant performance
item.description: The Canberra Raiders secured a hard-fought 26-22 victory over the Melbourne Storm in Round 7 of the NRL at Canberra's Bruce Stadium, in front of 17,321 fans. Centre Simi Sasagi was the standout perfor...

item.title: Blackmagic Design Unveils HyperDeck ISO Recorder 100G for SMPTE 2110 IP Video Recording
item.description: Blackmagic Design has introduced the HyperDeck ISO Recorder 100G, a powerful new SMPTE 2110 IP video recorder designed for modern live production workflows. The device records up to eight channels of ...

item.title: Bushra Bibi undergoes eye surgery for retinal detachment and returns to Adiala Jail
item.description: Bushra Bibi, wife of former prime minister Imran Khan and PTI founder, was shifted back to Adiala Jail in Rawalpindi on Friday after undergoing successful eye surgery at a private hospital. According ...

item.title: Joint Police and Military Operation Neutralizes 10 Dawlah Islamiya Members in Marantao, Lanao del Sur
item.description: In a joint operation early Friday morning on April 17, 2026, members of the Philippine National Police and the Philippine Army killed 10 remnants of the Dawlah Islamiya group in Barangay Banga-Pantar,...

item.title: Multiple African News Highlights: Zimbabwe Bus Explosion, Pope's Cameroon Visit, Activist Arrest and More
item.description: On 17 April 2026, several significant events unfolded across the African continent. In Zimbabwe, at least 18 people lost their lives when a commuter omnibus caught fire and exploded along the busy Bul...

item.title: Panthers Face Injury Woes as Garner Leaves the Field
item.description: The Penrith Panthers are in a tough spot after losing Luke Garner to injury during their recent NRL match. With the team already dealing with several key injuries, the loss of Garner adds to their mou...

item.title: Police Investigate Suspicious Items in Kensington Gardens Following Drone Footage Claim
item.description: Kensington Gardens has been closed to the public as police investigate suspicious items discovered in the area. The closure follows a video circulating online, in which a group claimed responsibility ...

item.title: Ruto Signs Law to Cut Fuel VAT by 50% for 90 Days
item.description: President William Ruto has signed the VAT (Amendment) Act 2026 into law, slashing the Value Added Tax (VAT) on fuel from 16% to 8% for a period of 90 days. The move is aimed at providing temporary rel...

item.title: Tanzania’s New Public Investment Bill to Enhance Efficiency of State Entities
item.description: The Tanzanian government plans to finalize the Public Investment Bill during the 2026/27 financial year, aiming to improve the performance of public entities and align them with the country’s Dira 205...

item.title: Akon Explains His Views On Men, Commitment, And Faithfulness
item.description: Senegalese singer Akon don open mouth talk about how men dey relate to monogamy and commitment. For one interview with The Shade Room, the 'Smack That' singer yan say men no dey naturally built to rem...

item.title: US Enforcement Actions Against Venezuelan Oil Shipments Impact Cuba's Fuel Supply
item.description: According to the Sky News article, the United States under President Donald Trump has implemented measures to intercept and disrupt Venezuelan oil shipments destined for Cuba. At the beginning of 2026...

item.title: Prince remembered by friends and collaborators a decade after his death
item.description: Ten years after Prince’s death, friends, collaborators and family members reflect on the life, talent and complexity of one of music’s most distinctive artists. George Clinton recalls recognising Prin...

item.title: President Samia Directs Ocean Road Cancer Institute to Maintain Advanced Cancer Diagnostic Equipment
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has given clear instructions to the management of the Ocean Road Cancer Institute (ORCI) in Dar es Salaam to ensure proper and regular maintenance of modern diagnostic ma...

item.title: Security Forces Heighten Alert in BARMM Ahead of First Parliamentary Elections in September
item.description: The Philippine government is strengthening security measures across the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) as preparations intensify for the region's first parliamentary elections...

item.title: Anthony Olanrewaju Recognised for Impactful Leadership in Nigeria’s Tech Scene
item.description: Anthony Olanrewaju Aderinwale, wey be co-founder and CEO of Hermplify Technologies Ltd., don make Nigeria Top 100 Persons of the Year list by 40Under40 Nigeria. Dis recognition dey celebrate people we...

item.title: Pierre Poilievre’s attacks on Mark Carney reveal deeper issues for the Conservative Party
item.description: In this opinion piece, National affairs columnist Gary Mason argues that the federal Conservative Party’s primary challenge is not the new Liberal majority government under Prime Minister Mark Carney,...

item.title: NSW Police disperse protesters at demonstration against Israeli President Isaac Herzog's visit to Sydney
item.description: On 17 April 2026, New South Wales Police dispersed crowds of protesters in Sydney during a demonstration related to Israeli President Isaac Herzog's visit to Australia. Footage captured by the Sydney ...

item.title: Keir Starmer faces growing pressure over Peter Mandelson security vetting failure
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has come under intense pressure following revelations that Peter Mandelson was appointed as US ambassador despite failing security vetting. Starmer insisted he was not info...
